question: QatarEnergy announces/resumes LNG production in Qatar by April 30?
description:
QatarEnergy, a Qatari state-owned petroleum company, announced on March 2 that it would halt production of liquefied natural gas (LNG) due to military strikes on its operating facilities (see: https://www.qatarenergy.qa/en/MediaCenter/Pages/newsdetails.aspx?ItemId=3892).

This market will resolve to “Yes” if QatarEnergy resumes production of liquefied natural gas at QatarEnergy LNG production facilities in Qatar, or officially announces that such production has resumed or will resume, by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

QatarEnergy resuming the production of other halted products, including downstream LNG-related products, or resuming transportation of LNG without resuming production, will not alone count.

An official announcement that LNG production will resume at QatarEnergy LNG production facilities in Qatar must signal the end of the total LNG production halt effective immediately or on a specific date or clearly defined time window. Mere statements that production will resume at some undefined point in the future, or that production will resume once the halt has ended, will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from QatarEnergy (https://www.qatarenergy.qa/en/Pages/vHome.aspx); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: QatarEnergy's preparations to restart LNG production at Ras Laffan facilities following a March 2026 halt due to military attacks on key sites and subsequent force majeure declaration have driven trader consensus to near-certainty on resumption or announcement by April 30. Post-ceasefire developments in early April, including Reuters reports of mobilizing engineers, workers, and restarting two trains at QELNG North 1, signal de-escalation in regional tensions around the Strait of Hormuz, boosting confidence despite gradual scaling projected over months. With probabilities exceeding 90%, resolution hinges on an official QatarEnergy statement confirming end of total halt; potential disputes over partial operations, ongoing force majeure, or lack of primary source verification could still prompt a No outcome.

question: Will Bitcoin hit $150k by June 30, 2026?
description:
This market will immediately resolve to "Yes" if any Binance 1 minute candle for Bitcoin (BTC/USDT) has a final "High" price equal to or greater than the price specified in the title by 11:59PM ET on the date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "High" prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT, with the chart settings on "1m" for one-minute candles selected on the top bar.

Please note that the outcome of this market depends solely on the price data from the Binance BTC/USDT trading pair. Prices from other exchanges, different trading pairs, or spot markets will not be considered for the resolution of this market.

question: Will AC Milan win on 2026-05-03?
description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for May 3, 2026
If AC Milan wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: US Sassuolo Calcio vs. AC Milan
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Serie A game, scheduled for Sunday, May 3, 2026 between US Sassuolo Calcio and AC Milan.

eventMetadata.context_description: AC Milan's deepening injury crisis, highlighted by Luka Modric's season-ending facial fracture suffered in their recent  Serie A draw against Juventus, has propelled US Sassuolo Calcio to a commanding 70.5% implied probability as home favorites at Citta del Tricolore. Sassuolo, 10th in the table with 41 goals scored and a resilient defensive setup conceding just once in their last outing—a goalless draw at Fiorentina—benefit from a healthier squad despite absences like Daniel Boloca and Fali Candè. Milan, third overall but leaking momentum away, field a patchwork lineup with Rafael Leão on limited training, Christian Pulisic rusty post-international duty, and long-term absentee Santiago Giménez, tilting trader consensus toward the hosts' home advantage and recent solidity over the Rossoneri's depleted attack. Draw priced at 20.5% reflects potential low-scoring stalemate risks.

events.title: Manchester United FC vs. Liverpool FC
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Premier League game, scheduled for Sunday, May 3, 2026 between Manchester United FC and Liverpool FC.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the high-stakes Premier League North West Derby at Old Trafford, Manchester United's 39.5% implied probability edges Liverpool's 34.5% amid a tight top-four battle—United third on 61 points needing just two more for Champions League qualification, Liverpool fourth on 58. Liverpool's Mohamed Salah is confirmed out with a hamstring tweak from their recent Crystal Palace win, alongside Alisson Becker and several others like Hugo Ekitike, weakening their attack; United counters defensive woes without Matthijs de Ligt (back) and suspended Lisandro Martinez. question: Indian Premier League: Sunrisers Hyderabad vs Kolkata Knight Riders
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Sunrisers Hyderabad and Kolkata Knight Riders scheduled for May 3 2026 in Indian Premier League.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Sunrisers Hyderabad's five-match winning streak, including chases of 220+ against Mumbai Indians and Rajasthan Royals, positions them strongly atop trader consensus at home in Hyderabad, where the Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium's flat, batting-friendly pitch with quick outfield favors high-scoring T20 run-fests and second-innings pursuits. Kolkata Knight Riders, buoyed by back-to-back wins after a slow start (two victories in eight), seek to exploit SRH's occasional middle-order wobbles via spinners Sunil Narine and Varun Chakaravarthy against explosive openers Travis Head and Abhishek Sharma (425 runs in nine innings). SRH's earlier 65-run thumping of KKR this season underscores their edge, though clear skies and 34-42°C heat pose no disruptions; toss winner likely bats second.

question: Will the Iranian regime fall by May 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Islamic Republic of Iran’s current ruling regime is overthrown, collapsed, or otherwise ceases to govern by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This requires a broad consensus of reporting indicating that core structures of the Islamic Republic (e.g. the office of the Supreme Leader, the Guardian Council, IRGC control under clerical authority) have been dissolved, incapacitated, or replaced by a fundamentally different governing system or otherwise lost de facto power over a majority of the population of Iran. This could occur via revolution, civil war, military coup, or voluntary abdication, but only qualifies if the Islamic Republic no longer exercises sovereign power.

Routine political events such as elections, reforms, or leadership succession do not qualify. Internal coups or power shifts that preserve the Islamic Republic’s core structures also do not qualify. Only a clear break in continuity—such as a new provisional government, revolutionary council, or constitution replacing the Islamic Republic will qualify.

Partial loss of territory or challenges from rebel or exile groups will not qualify unless the Islamic Republic no longer administers the majority of the Iranian population within Iran. 

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

This could occur via revolution, civil war, military coup, or voluntary abdication, but only qualifies if the Islamic Republic no longer exercises sovereign power.

Routine political events such as elections, reforms, or leadership succession do not qualify. Internal coups or power shifts that preserve the Islamic Republic’s core structures also do not qualify. eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects near-certainty that the Iranian regime will endure through May 31, driven by its demonstrated resilience amid the 2026 Iran war and prior unrest. Security forces loyal to the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) effectively suppressed the 2025–2026 protests via mass crackdowns and internet blackouts, quelling thousands of deaths without defections or loss of major cities. Following Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei's February assassination, Mojtaba Khamenei assumed leadership seamlessly by March, stabilizing command structures despite economic devastation from US naval blockades and $270 billion in war damage. A tense US-Iran ceasefire since early April holds, with no unified opposition or indicators of collapse per US intelligence assessments. Realistic shifts could arise from IRGC infighting, resumed large-scale airstrikes, or sudden mass defections, though the 28-day timeline limits feasibility.

question: Will Croatia win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: France holds a narrow trader consensus edge at 16.4% implied probability to win the 2026 FIFA World Cup, buoyed by squad depth under Didier Deschamps despite losing Hugo Ekitike to Achilles injury, with Kylian Mbappé at peak age and recent Nations League form solidifying their back-to-back finalists status. Spain trails closely at 15.3% on Euro championship momentum and La Masia youth pipeline, though Lamine Yamal's fitness race tempers enthusiasm. England (11.1%), Argentina, and Brazil (both 8.6%) remain viable amid Rodrygo and Éder Militão absences for Brazil, keeping the top bunch tight in the 48-team expanded format where group draw paths, North American travel, and summer heat amplify upset potential across elite contenders.

question: LoL: ThunderTalk Gaming vs LNG Esports (BO3) - LPL Group Nirvana
description:
This market refers to the LoL match between ThunderTalk Gaming and LNG Esports in the LPL Group Nirvana, initially scheduled for May 3 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "ThunderTalk Gaming" if ThunderTalk Gaming win the match against LNG Esports.

This market will resolve to "LNG Esports" if LNG Esports win the match against ThunderTalk Gaming.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: In LPL 2026 Split 2 Group Nirvana's single round-robin, ThunderTalk Gaming (2-1, second place) faces LNG Esports (1-1, fourth) in a pivotal BO3 for top-four spots advancing to playoffs play-in, with bottom two eliminated. TT enters off a 1-2 loss to leader LGD Gaming on April 30—dominating map one 21-3 before faltering—following 2-0 over Ultra Prime and 2-1 versus Oh My God, showcasing midlaner Heru's carry potential amid their rebuilt 2026 roster. LNG rides a 2-0 rout of struggling Oh My God last week after a 1-2 EDG defeat, leveraging jungler Croco's tempo control despite historical 9-5 H2H edge (TT won latest March 28 clash 2-0). Patch 26.7 favors aggressive early plays, amplifying macro execution in this do-or-die group stage matchup.

question: Raptors vs. Cavaliers
description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for May 3 at 12:00AM ET:

eventMetadata.context_description: The Cleveland Cavaliers host the Toronto Raptors in a decisive Eastern Conference first-round Game 7 today at Rocket Mortgage FieldHouse, with the series tied 3-3 after Toronto's dramatic 112-110 overtime victory in Game 6 on May 1, capped by RJ Barrett's bouncing game-winning 3-pointer. Cavaliers enter fully healthy per the latest injury report, leveraging home-court advantage, superior regular-season record (52-30 vs. 46-36), and stars like Donovan Mitchell and James Harden, while Raptors are without Immanuel Quickley (right hamstring strain) and list Brandon Ingram as questionable (right heel inflammation). Toronto's resilience without key pieces and Scottie Barnes' two-way impact fuel upset potential, but Cleveland's depth and 15-5 all-time playoff edge against Toronto shape trader consensus toward the hosts.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder command 57.5% implied probability as defending champions and Western Conference top seed after a dominant 4-0 first-round sweep of the Suns, now facing the Lakers—who grinded out a 4-3 series win over the Rockets—in the conference semifinals, with their path eased by Denver's seeding. San Antonio Spurs hold 20.1% following a straightforward 4-1 dismissal of the Trail Blazers, positioning Victor Wembanyama's squad for a likely clash with Minnesota or Denver next and a potential Western Conference Finals rematch against OKC. Eastern chaos, including Game 7s for top-seeded Pistons versus Magic (3-3) and Cavaliers-Raptors, plus Knicks' series lead over Hawks and 76ers' rally against Celtics, elevates West frontrunners amid no clear Eastern powerhouse.

question: LoL: DN SOOPers vs BNK FEARX - Game 2 Winner

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without play beginning, this market will resolve 50-50.

events.title: LoL: DN SOOPers vs BNK FEARX (BO3) - LCK Rounds 1-2
events.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between DN SOOPers and BNK FEARX in the LCK Rounds 1-2, initially scheduled for May 3 at 6:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "DN SOOPers" if DN SOOPers win the match against BNK FEARX.

This market will resolve to "BNK FEARX" if BNK FEARX win the match against DN SOOPers.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: BNK FEARX holds the edge in trader consensus for this LCK Rounds 1-2 BO3 series against DN SOOPers, buoyed by their clean 2-0 sweep over the same opponent in Week 2 and mid-table positioning amid DNS's grueling 16-series losing streak, including recent 0-2 defeats to HANJIN BRION and Gen.G. DN SOOPers' core roster—DuDu top, Pyosik jungle, Clozer mid, deokdam ADC, Peter support—struggles with macro execution and objective control, while BNK FEARX leverages Kellin's jungle pressure and VicLa's mid-lane stability in the current patch's skirmish-heavy meta. Head-to-head history and DNS's bottom-tier form underscore BNK's stylistic matchup advantages, though upsets remain possible in double round-robin play with Road to MSI implications looming.

eventMetadata.context_description: Detroit Pistons host Orlando Magic in Game 7 of their Eastern Conference first-round playoff series, tied 3-3, with recent momentum swinging toward the No. 1 seed after a stunning 24-point comeback win in Game 6 (93-79 at Orlando). Cade Cunningham erupted for 32 points, dominating the fourth quarter as Magic's offense imploded (1-for-27 over final 16 minutes), erasing their 3-1 lead. Orlando rules out Franz Wagner (calf strain), a major scoring blow, with Jonathan Isaac doubtful (knee); Pistons list Kevin Huerter questionable (adductor) but clear Tobias Harris (ankle). Home-court advantage, Detroit's 60-22 regular-season dominance, and playoff resilience shape trader sentiment amid high-stakes pressure.

This BO3 rematch under Hard Fearless Draft emphasizes draft adaptability and map control in a high-stakes group stage clash.

question: Strait of Hormuz traffic returns to normal by May 15?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls (“Arrivals of Ships”) for the Strait of Hormuz equal to or above 60 for any date between market creation and May 15, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Daily transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls equal to or above the specified level, or once data has been published for the final date in the specified period and no such value has been published. If no data has been published for the final date of the specified period within 14 calendar days (ET) after the end of that period, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made within this market’s timeframe will be considered. However, they will not disqualify a previously published data point from qualifying. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for May 31, 2026, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ongoing US naval blockade of Iranian ports and Iran's retaliatory restrictions on Strait of Hormuz transits have kept commercial shipping traffic at under 10% of normal levels—fewer than 10 vessels daily versus the typical 140—driving trader consensus to 95.5% against normalization by May 15. Recent navigation data as of May 2 shows passage limited to select Iran-linked tankers amid IRGC enforcement and over 40 reported incidents since March, including harassment and near-misses per UKMTO. With no diplomatic breakthrough or ceasefire expansion despite stalled talks, the mutual standoff persists; only a sudden de-escalation, such as lifted blockades or multilateral negotiations, could shift odds, though historical precedents suggest prolonged disruptions in such crises.

question: US obtains Iranian enriched uranium by May 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the US government or military officially announces or confirms that it has gained possession of any quantity of enriched uranium previously controlled by Iran by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

“Possession” means that the United States has actual physical custody or control of the enriched uranium, whether held within U.S. territory or elsewhere. Announcements of deals, agreements, commitments, or plans under which the United States would acquire possession of Iranian enriched uranium at a later time will not qualify.

Qualifying possession of Iranian enriched uranium may be acquired through any means, including through an agreed surrender or seizure.

A widespread consensus of credible reporting that the United States has gained possession of Iranian enriched uranium will also qualify for a “Yes” resolution, even if the United States makes no formal announcement.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of the United States; however, a widespread consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Amid stalled US-Iran nuclear negotiations following February 2026 US-Israeli airstrikes on enrichment facilities, traders remain skeptical of near-term US acquisition of Iran's 11-ton enriched uranium stockpile, including 440 kilograms at near-weapons-grade 60% purity. IAEA February reports noted unverified underground storage at sites like Isfahan, complicating inspections post-"Midnight Hammer" operations. President Trump's April 29 rejection of Tehran's proposal—disputing a 20-year US-demanded enrichment suspension versus Iran's five-year offer—has prolonged a naval blockade, underscoring diplomatic impasse after an April 8 ceasefire. Upcoming Gulf talks represent a key catalyst, with historical JCPOA patterns suggesting fragile progress potential amid escalation risks.

eventMetadata.context_description: RC Celta de Vigo's commanding 2-0 lead deep into the second half at Balaídos has driven trader consensus to a 94.5% implied probability of a home win in this La Liga clash, reflecting their home advantage and first-half dominance despite a prior three-match losing streak. Seventh-placed Celta, with 44 points from 11 wins, outmuscle Elche—who trail by six points and seek a fourth straight victory—via superior quality and Elche's away defensive woes. Elche's slim 0.7% upset path or 4.5% draw hinges on a rapid two-goal rally, Celta expulsion, key injury like Matias Vecino's adductor issue worsening, or fatigue-exploiting counters in stoppage time.

eventMetadata.context_description: Daria Kasatkina, the No. 7 seed and world No. 75, holds a clear edge over Tamara Korpatsch (No. 99) in the WTA 125K La Bisbal d'Emporda clay-court final, their first head-to-head meeting. Kasatkina sharpened her form with a straight-sets semifinal win over Sara Sorribes Tormo after earlier triumphs against Emiliana Arango (6-2, 3-6, 7-6) and Laura Pigossi (6-2, 6-2), rebounding from a 4-6 start to the season. Korpatsch, boasting an 11-9 record in 2026, rallied from a set down to upset local favorite Bassols Ribas 3-6, 6-0, 6-4 in semis following a 2-0 quarterfinal win over Caroline Werner. On red clay favoring baseline rallies, Kasatkina's superior ranking and experience counter Korpatsch's hot streak through the draw.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices "No" at 81.5% on Strait of Hormuz traffic returning to normal by May 31, reflecting persistent disruptions from the US naval blockade imposed April 13—targeting Iran-bound vessels—and Iran's retaliatory restrictions amid the ongoing conflict. Shipping data shows transits at 5% of pre-February crisis averages, with only 3-14 commercial vessels daily versus the normal 120-140, mostly Iran-linked, as reported through May 1. A brief April 8 ceasefire collapsed after attacks on ships, while recent US sanctions warnings to firms paying Iranian tolls (May 3) and stalled diplomacy deepen the impasse. Normalization faces steep barriers from military posturing, though a breakthrough deal or de-escalation could shift odds rapidly.

events.title: AFC Bournemouth vs. Crystal Palace FC
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Premier League game, scheduled for Sunday, May 3, 2026 between AFC Bournemouth and Crystal Palace FC.

eventMetadata.context_description: AFC Bournemouth's Premier League-leading 14-game unbeaten streak (W6 D8), bolstered by just two home losses in 17 matches at Vitality Stadium, drives trader consensus toward an 82.5% implied probability of victory over Crystal Palace. The Cherries sit seventh with 49 points, chasing European qualification, and drew 2-2 at home to Leeds last weekend to extend the run, with near-full fitness aside from Lewis Cook's thigh issue and Justin Kluivert's knee doubt despite his training return. Palace, 13th on 43 points, lost 3-1 at Liverpool recently but progressed in the Conference League semi-final Thursday, risking rotation and fatigue; key absences include forwards Evann Guessand (knee), Eddie Nketiah (thigh), and midfielder Cheick Doucoure (muscle), exacerbating their seven losses in 13 away league games. Unbeaten in five head-to-heads (W2 D3), Bournemouth's momentum overshadows Palace's defensive organization.

question: US x Iran diplomatic meeting by May 5, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify. 

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not count.

The meeting must be in-person (including indirect in-person meetings) and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran, and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Amid fragile US-Iran ceasefire following recent hostilities over the Strait of Hormuz, indirect negotiations mediated in Pakistan stalled last week after Iran's foreign minister departed Islamabad without progress. Tehran submitted a new 14-point proposal yesterday prioritizing Hormuz reopening and truce extension before nuclear talks, which President Trump is reviewing but dismissed similar prior offers as insufficient. No direct diplomatic meetings have materialized despite earlier speculation involving US envoys like Kushner and Vance; Trump unilaterally prolonged the ceasefire amid exchanged threats and sanctions disputes. Traders monitor for potential resumption signals or situation room updates, as deadlock sustains uncertainty ahead of any escalation risks.

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

events.title: Counter-Strike: Natus Vincere vs Vitality (BO5) - BLAST Rivals Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Grand final match between Natus Vincere and Vitality in the BLAST Rivals Playoffs, initially scheduled for May 3 at 1:30PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Natus Vincere" if Natus Vincere win the match against Vitality.

This market will resolve to "Vitality" if Vitality win the match against Natus Vincere.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vitality and Natus Vincere meet in the BLAST Rivals Spring 2026 grand final BO5 at Dickies Arena after both swept semifinals 2-0 on May 2—NaVi crushing FaZe on Dust II and Ancient behind w0nderful's sharp rifling, while Vitality dismantled GamerLegion on Mirage and Overpass with ZywOo's dominance. As HLTV's #1 and #2 teams, Vitality holds a commanding head-to-head edge, including a 3-0 BO5 sweep over NaVi at BLAST Open Rotterdam last month and nine straight map wins against them, bolstered by superior past-three-month stats (1.44 rating, 89 ADR). NaVi's undefeated group stage run and Aleksib's in-game leadership signal upset potential in a high-stakes single-elimination finale with the standard map pool.
